title: ptiloria cichoriacea
intro: Ptiloria cichoriacea, also known as the parsnip flower or knawel, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Ptiloria cichoriacea are attributed to its seeds, which contain a bitter compound that has been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of consuming Ptiloria cichoriacea include its ability to aid digestion, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Ptiloria cichoriacea is often prepared as a tincture or infused oil, and it can also be consumed as a tea or added to food products, showcasing its versatility in herbal medicine.
